Checks.PassMessage: PDC Emulator is synchronized to a reliable external time source
Checks.FailMessage: PDC Emulator time source issues detected

EvaluationRules.Message: PDC Emulator has no external NTP time source configured
PDC time source check reported failure

Checks.Help: Verifies the PDC Emulator is synchronized to a reliable external NTP time source. The PDC is the authoritative time source for the entire domain - incorrect PDC time propagates to all DCs and clients, potentially breaking Kerberos.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Configure the PDC to sync from reliable external NTP: w32tm /config /manualpeerlist:'pool.ntp.org,0x8' /syncfromflags:MANUAL /reliable:YES /update. 2. Restart W32Time: Restart-Service W32Time. 3. Force sync: w32tm /resync /force. 4. Verify: w32tm /query /computer:<PDC> /status.

Checks.PassMessage: All DCs are synchronized within acceptable time offset limits
Checks.FailMessage: Time synchronization issues detected on domain controllers

EvaluationRules.Message: DC time offset exceeds 5 minutes - Kerberos authentication will fail
DC time offset exceeds 1 minute - approaching Kerberos tolerance limit
DC time offset issue detected

Checks.Help: Measures the time difference between each DC and a reference time source. Kerberos authentication fails if any DC's clock differs by more than 5 minutes from the domain time, causing widespread login failures.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Check current offset: w32tm /stripchart /computer:<PDC> /dataonly /samples:3. 2. Force resync: w32tm /resync /force on the affected DC. 3. If offset is large, set time manually then resync: Set-Date, then w32tm /resync. 4. Ensure W32Time service is running and set to Automatic: Set-Service W32Time -StartupType Automatic.

Checks.PassMessage: All configured NTP servers are reachable
Checks.FailMessage: One or more NTP servers are unreachable

EvaluationRules.Message: One or more configured NTP servers are unreachable on UDP port 123

Checks.Help: Tests UDP port 123 connectivity to all NTP servers configured on the PDC Emulator. If the PDC cannot reach its configured NTP servers, it falls back to the local clock, causing domain-wide time drift.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Test NTP reachability: Test-NetConnection -ComputerName pool.ntp.org -Port 123. 2. Check firewall rules on the DC and perimeter - allow outbound UDP 123. 3. If servers are unreachable, configure alternative NTP servers: w32tm /config /manualpeerlist:'0.pool.ntp.org 1.pool.ntp.org'. 4. Consider using Windows Time service with the VM host clock as backup.

Checks.PassMessage: All DCs have correct W32Time provider configuration

EvaluationRules.Message: One or more DCs have incorrect W32Time Type configuration (PDC should be NTP, others NT5DS)
W32Time provider configuration issue detected

Checks.Help: Verifies W32Time is configured correctly: the PDC should use NTP type (syncing from external source) and all other DCs should use NT5DS type (syncing from the domain hierarchy).
Checks.Remediation: 1. On the PDC Emulator run: w32tm /config /manualpeerlist:'pool.ntp.org,0x8' /syncfromflags:MANUAL /reliable:YES /update. 2. On all other DCs: w32tm /config /syncfromflags:DOMHIER /reliable:NO /update. 3. Restart W32Time on all DCs: Restart-Service W32Time. 4. Verify type: w32tm /query /configuration | findstr Type.

Checks.PassMessage: W32Time service is running and set to Auto on all DCs
Checks.FailMessage: W32Time service issues detected on one or more DCs

EvaluationRules.Message: W32Time service is not running on one or more DCs
W32Time service is not set to automatic startup on one or more DCs
W32Time service issue detected

Checks.Help: Checks that the Windows Time service is running and set to automatic startup on all DCs. A stopped W32Time service causes the DC to stop synchronizing time, eventually drifting beyond Kerberos tolerance.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Start the service: Start-Service W32Time. 2. Set to automatic: Set-Service W32Time -StartupType Automatic. 3. Re-register W32Time: w32tm /unregister; w32tm /register; Start-Service W32Time. 4. Check if a GPO is setting W32Time startup type - verify in GPMC.

Checks.PassMessage: All DCs have acceptable NTP stratum levels
Checks.FailMessage: NTP stratum level issues detected on one or more DCs

EvaluationRules.Message: One or more DCs have stratum 16 - clock is unsynchronized
Stratum level issue detected on one or more DCs

Checks.Help: Checks the NTP stratum level of each DC. Stratum 16 means the clock is unsynchronized. High stratum levels indicate the DC is many hops from a reliable time source, reducing accuracy.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Stratum 16 means W32Time is not syncing - run w32tm /resync /force. 2. If the PDC has a high stratum, configure an external NTP source (see TIME-001). 3. Check: w32tm /query /status on the affected DC. 4. Ensure W32Time can reach its configured source - check network/firewall.

Checks.PassMessage: NTP poll interval is configured within acceptable limits on all PDC Emulators
Checks.FailMessage: NTP poll interval is too long on one or more PDC Emulators

EvaluationRules.Message: PDC SpecialPollInterval exceeds 1 day - time corrections are too infrequent
PDC SpecialPollInterval exceeds 1 hour - consider reducing for more frequent sync
Poll interval issue detected

Checks.Help: Checks how frequently the PDC polls its NTP time source. A very long poll interval (over 1 hour) means the PDC is infrequently correcting its clock, allowing gradual drift.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Reduce SpecialPollInterval: w32tm /config /SpecialPollInterval:3600 /update. 2. Typical recommended value is 3600 seconds (1 hour) for the PDC. 3. Restart W32Time after changes: Restart-Service W32Time. 4. Verify: w32tm /query /configuration | findstr SpecialPollInterval.

Checks.PassMessage: Phase offsets are within acceptable limits on all DCs
Checks.FailMessage: Excessive phase offset detected on one or more DCs

EvaluationRules.Message: DC phase offset exceeds 60 seconds - Kerberos authentication may fail
DC phase offset exceeds 1 second - monitor for further drift
Phase offset issue detected

Checks.Help: Monitors the phase offset (pending time correction) on each DC. A large phase offset means the DC clock is significantly off and W32Time is working to correct it, which may cause temporary Kerberos issues.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Force immediate resync: w32tm /resync /force on the affected DC. 2. If offset is very large (over 60 seconds), manually set the time first then resync. 3. Investigate why the clock drifted - check for suspended VMs or failed NTP sync. 4. Review w32tm /query /status to confirm correction is in progress.

Checks.PassMessage: All DCs have consistent time zone configuration
Checks.FailMessage: Time zone inconsistency detected across domain controllers

EvaluationRules.Message: DCs have inconsistent time zones - log correlation issues may occur
One or more DCs are not configured for UTC - UTC is recommended for domain controllers

Checks.Help: Verifies all DCs use the same time zone. Mixed time zones cause confusing event log timestamps and complicate cross-DC log correlation during security investigations.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Standardize all DCs to UTC: Set-TimeZone -Id 'UTC' (requires reboot or service restart to take effect). 2. UTC is recommended for DCs to eliminate DST complications. 3. Change via Server Manager or: tzutil /s 'UTC'. 4. Update monitoring tools to display times in UTC for consistency.

Checks.PassMessage: DST configuration is consistent across all DCs
Checks.FailMessage: Inconsistent DST configuration detected across domain controllers

EvaluationRules.Message: DCs have inconsistent DST settings - some DCs observe DST and others do not
One or more DCs observe DST - UTC with no DST is recommended to prevent time jumps

Checks.Help: Checks Daylight Saving Time configuration across DCs. DCs observing DST experience time jumps twice per year that can briefly interrupt Kerberos authentication and cause log timestamp confusion.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Set all DCs to UTC to eliminate DST: tzutil /s 'UTC' on each DC. 2. UTC has no DST transitions - clocks never jump. 3. If UTC is not possible, ensure all DCs use the same DST-observing time zone. 4. Schedule DC maintenance windows to avoid DST changeover weekends.
